standards_summary.source: Draft SReq (Feb 2026) — 4 standards, all listed individually

content.about: Covers factory-made precast concrete elements such as hollow core slabs, beams, wall and facade panels, stairs, masts, bridge elements, and box culverts — in normal-weight, lightweight aggregate and autoclaved aerated concrete. Products range from simple unreinforced items to prestressed structural members. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 229 (precast concrete products), with CEN/TC 177 contributing standards for lightweight aggregate and autoclaved aerated concrete products.
content.standards_landscape: EN 1520:2011 (lightweight aggregate, AVCP System 2+), EN 12602:2016 (autoclaved aerated concrete, AVCP System 2+). Both cited in OJ under old CPR 305/2011 via mandate M/100 (1994). EN 13369:2023 (common rules; national adoptions 2024) replacing EN 13369:2018 — this is a non-harmonised reference standard (no Annex ZA), but underpins all precast product standards. AVCP per Commission Decision 1999/94/EC (System 2+ for structural, System 4 for non-structural).
content.standards_development: prEN 12602 — draft standard dated 24 July 2025 (BSI reference 25/30510163 DC), confirming revision already underway under CEN/TC 177. EN 1170:2024 (test methods for fibre-reinforced precast) published Dec 2024 — supporting standard development is active.
content.sreq_analysis: Acquis Milestone I (product scope) and Milestone III (essential characteristics) both completed. Draft implementing decision published 4 Feb 2026 via Art. 12 notification (earlier draft from April 2024). Feedback until 3 Mar 2026. Under CPR 305/2011 (old regulation). Replaces mandate M/100 (1994). 4 deliverables, all due 30 Oct 2026: (1) new hEN for precast concrete performance assessment (Table 1), (2) EPD product category rules for lightweight and AAC products (Table 1), (3) revision of EN 1520:2011 (Table 2), (4) revision of EN 12602:2016 (Table 2). Decision expires 30 Jun 2027. CEN reports due 31 Jan 2027. Table 3 target of SReq Q2 2025 was missed by ~8 months. NOT yet adopted — CEN WP 2025 still lists as "M/XXX" (no mandate number assigned).
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2030–2031, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request draft published (Feb 2026) and delivery expected Q4 2026, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2030–2031. Note: this standardisation request is under CPR 305/2011 – a subsequent request under CPR 2024/3110 is needed before DPP applies. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 4 hENs range from ~2030 to ~2031 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). Both scenarios converge on the same timeline. GCCA Low Carbon Ratings (7-band AA–G, launched Apr 2025) explicitly includes precast concrete. Provides voluntary carbon class overlay alongside mandatory CPR GWP.
content.stakeholder_notes: CEN/TC 229 (EN 13369, EN 1520) and CEN/TC 177 (EN 12602) are active. BIBM Decarbonisation Pledge. GCCA Low Carbon Ratings (7-band AA–G, Apr 2025) explicitly includes precast. ReCreate (H2020) demonstrates 93–98% energy/carbon savings from reusing intact precast components, with EPDs for reused products.
content.key_risks: (1) SReq feedback deadline 3 Mar 2026 — monitor for adoption. (2) EN 13369 is a non-harmonised reference standard underpinning multiple precast hENs. (3) GWP declarations mandatory from 8 Jan 2026 for EN 1520 and EN 12602 products (OJ-cited under M/100) — early compliance burden before DPP system is operational. (4) Future new-CPR SReq may cover all 24 hENs (not just 4 currently tracked).
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 CPR Working Plan [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; nlfnorm.cz hEN database [S143]; EOTA EAD database [S144]; SReq notification system Art. 12; BIBM; GCCA LCR.

content.about: Covers structural steel and aluminium products used in load-bearing construction, including hot-rolled and cold-formed sections, stainless steel structural members, high-strength and non-preloaded bolting assemblies, welding consumables, and insulating sandwich panels. Conformity assessment centres on the execution of steel and aluminium structures and their components. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 135.
content.standards_landscape: EN 1090-1:2009+A1:2011 is the primary conformity assessment standard, but the OJ-cited harmonised standards for this family also include EN 15088:2005, EN 10340:2007, EN 14399-1:2015, EN 15048-1:2007, EN 13479:2017, EN 10025-1:2004, EN 10210-1:2006, EN 10219-1:2006, EN 10088-4:2009, and EN 10088-5:2009 (all under mandate M/120 and CPR 305/2011). Supported by execution standards EN 1090-2:2018+A1:2024 (steel, sustainability provisions added), EN 1090-3:2019 (aluminium), EN 1090-4:2018 and EN 1090-5:2017 (cold-formed). CEN/TS 1090-201:2024 covers structural steel reuse.
content.standards_development: The European standardisation mandate for the EN 1090-1 revision was rejected by a majority of EU member states. Concerns raised: unclear scope, undefined terminology, unnecessary/missing performance characteristics (welding suitability, fracture toughness, fatigue strength removed). CEN development stage dropped from 40.60 to 10.99. A revised mandate is expected early 2026. Publication now estimated ~2028–2029 at earliest. (per TC 135 work programme):• prEN 1090-1 rev — mandate rejected, back to stage 10.99• prEN 1090-4 rev — cold-formed steel (stage 40.60, vote May 2026, NOT harmonised under CPR)• prEN 1090-5 rev — cold-formed aluminium (stage 40.60, vote Jul 2026, NOT harmonised under CPR)• prEN 17662 — EPD PCR for steel/iron/aluminium (stage 45.99, FV dispatched Dec 2023 — possibly stalled) CEN/TS 1090-201:2024 published — first European technical specification for reuse of structural steel. Not harmonised but establishes technical framework for future SReq incorporation.
content.sreq_analysis: Acquis Milestone I (product scope) and Milestone III (essential characteristics) both completed. Commission Implementing Decision C(2025) 6586, adopted 2 October 2025 under CPR 305/2011 (replacing mandate M/120). 12 standards (5 new, 7 revisions), all due 15 November 2025 — deadline missed (~6 weeks from adoption). Decision expires 31 Jan 2027. COM(2025) 772 confirms SMP as first-wave priority for CPR 2024/3110. New SReq under new CPR planned Q2 2026 (delivery Q3 2027).
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2030–2032, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request adopted (Oct 2025) and delivery expected 15 Nov 2025 (overdue), the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2031–2032. Note: this standardisation request is under CPR 305/2011 – a subsequent request under CPR 2024/3110 is needed before DPP applies. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 12 hENs and 28 EADs range from ~2030 to ~2032 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). The combined range spans from the standards-based assessment’s earlier estimate (~2030–2032) to the policy pipeline’s later horizon (~2031–2032).
content.stakeholder_notes: 28+ EADs under Product Area 20: Structural steel sections (EAD 200017, 200022, 200116), tension rods/cables (EAD 200001, 200002, 200006, 200032), piles/foundations (EAD 200005, 200014, 200036, 200043, 200077), gabions/baskets (EAD 200019, 200020, 200026, 200039), connections (EAD 200033, 200086, 200102), corrosion protection (EAD 200126, 200188), and others. Previously reported as 0 EADs. Note: EADs 200036, 200043, 200077, 200039, 200086, 200102, 200116, 200126, 200188 referenced in stakeholder context but not verified against EOTA PA 20 scope. Data audit pending.
content.key_risks: (1) EN 1090-1 mandate rejection (summer 2025) — major bottleneck. Revised mandate expected early 2026 but publication now ~2028–2029 at earliest. (2) SReq delivery deadline missed (15 Nov 2025) — CEN has not yet delivered. (3) 28 legacy EADs expire 9 Jan 2031 — no transition planning started. (4) New SReq under CPR 2024/3110 planned Q2 2026 — adds second parallel standards development track. (5) COM(2025) 726 Steel & Metals Action Plan: CBAM + ESPR + safeguard tariffs create multi-layered compliance environment. (6) ESPR iron & steel delegated act (~2027 compliance) may create an ESPR DPP before CPR DPP — dual regulatory pathway.

standards_summary.source: SReq C(2025) 4828 Annex I + EOTA database

content.about: Covers the full range of cements, building limes, and hydraulic binders used in construction, from common Portland cements and masonry cements to specialist types such as calcium aluminate, supersulphated, and very low heat cements, as well as hydraulic road binders. Cement is explicitly carved out of the ESPR, meaning sustainability requirements are set under the CPR instead. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 51.
content.standards_landscape: EN 197-1:2011 (common cement), EN 14216:2015, EN 413-1:2011, EN 14647:2005, EN 15743:2010+A1:2015, EN 459-1:2010, EN 13282-1:2013, EN 15368:2008+A1:2010. All cited under old CPR 305/2011 via mandate M/114 (1997). GWP declarations mandatory from 8 Jan 2026 (Annex II). CBAM financial obligations from 1 Jan 2026. EAD 150080-00-0301 (blended cements with reduced clinker content) formally cited in OJEU December 2025 — milestone for low-carbon cement technologies. Ecocem holds ETA 23-0877 (ACT technology, issued by Cerema Dec 2023) and is building a EUR 50M production facility in Dunkirk (300kt/yr capacity, commercial operation H2 2026). Commission signalled Feb 2025 that an EOTA mandate for alkali-activated cements EAD could follow by 2027.
content.standards_development: prEN 197-1 rev — new project approved 2 July 2025 by CEN/TC 51 (stage 10.99). Revision will integrate EN 197-5 (CEM II/C-M and CEM VI low-carbon cements) and EN 197-6 (cement with recycled building materials), enabling wider range of low-carbon cements. EN 196-12:2024 published Dec 2024 (reactivity test methods supporting performance-based approach). NOTE: Only EN 197-1 has a registered CEN prEN project. All other 8 standards covered by C(2025) 4828 have no registered prEN yet — risk for 2027-2028 deadlines.
content.sreq_analysis: Acquis Milestone I (product scope) and Milestone III (essential characteristics) both completed. Commission Implementing Decision C(2025) 4828, adopted 28 July 2025. Confirmed under CPR 2024/3110 (title: "in support of Regulation (EU) 2024/3110"). DPP applies directly. Replaces mandate M/114 (expired 31 Dec 2025). 10 deliverables — 1 new TR + 9 revisions (Annex I). Deadlines: EN 197-1 + EN 16908 by 30 Jun 2027; EN 413-1, EN 459-1, EN 15368, alkali-activated TR by 31 Dec 2027; EN 14216, EN 14647, EN 15743, EN 13282 by 30 Jun 2028. Decision expires 30 Jun 2030.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2029–2032, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request adopted (Jul 2025) and delivery expected Jun 2027–Jun 2028, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2029–2030. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 9 hENs and 1 EAD range from ~2029 to ~2032 (8 of 9 hENs under CPR 305/2011, needing revision under CPR 2024/3110). The combined range spans from the standards-based assessment’s earlier estimate (~2029–2032) to the policy pipeline’s later horizon (~2029–2030).
content.stakeholder_notes: Alkali-activated cements: Downgraded to Technical Report in C(2025) 4828, despite strong stakeholder opposition (19 signatories). CEN/TC 51 refused to develop standards. A performance-based standard for alternative low-carbon cements requested for delivery by 2030.SReq C(2025) 4828 lifecycle: expires 30 Jun 2030. CEN first annual report due 30 Jun 2026 — key data point for standards development progress monitoring. Industrial Decarbonisation Accelerator Act (IDAA): postponed from Dec 2025, will operationalise cement carbon label mandating GWP disclosure (kgCO₂ eq./t) in Declaration of Performance via CPR. EPBD requires lifecycle GWP for buildings >1000m² from 2028, all new buildings from 2030. Germany already has national cement carbon labelling. Cement Europe (formerly CEMBUREAU) supports lead markets approach. EU ETS/CBAM interaction: From 1 Jan 2026, ETS free allocations shift to binder-based benchmark (Cement Europe flags as ‘unclear’). Free allocation phase-out: 97.5% remaining 2026, 51.5% by 2030, 0% by 2034. CBAM definitive compliance started 1 Jan 2026. Progressive carbon cost makes CPR GWP disclosure on DPPs economically consequential — low-carbon cements gain real cost advantage.
content.key_risks: (1) Standards development bottleneck: Only EN 197-1 has a registered prEN (stage 10.99, Jul 2025). The remaining 8 product standards (EN 413-1, EN 459-1, EN 15368, EN 14216, EN 14647, EN 15743, EN 13282, EN 16908) have SReq delivery deadlines in 2027–2028 but no prEN registrations yet. CEN first annual report (due Jun 2026) will clarify progress. (2) Alkali-activated cements: CEN/TC 51 refused to develop standards; downgraded to Technical Report in C(2025) 4828. Performance-based standard requested for delivery by 2030 but uncertain. (3) EAD sunset risk: EAD 150080-00-0301 (blended cements) valid only until 9 Jan 2031 under old CPR. New-regime EAD needed for continued ETA route. EOTA mandate by 2027 signaled but not yet formalised. (4) Clean Industrial Deal: Mandatory cement carbon label (GWP disclosure in DoP) planned when new standards become mandatory, adding compliance complexity. (5) EPBD GWP demand: Commission delegated regulation C(2025) 8723 (16 Dec 2025) establishes building life-cycle GWP calculations: >1000m² from Jan 2028, all new buildings from Jan 2030. Data hierarchy favours product-specific EPDs, creating downstream pull for plant-specific digital EPD data aligned with DPP requirements. (6) EN 197-5 gap: EN 197-5:2021 (CEM II/C-M, CEM VI ternary cements, ~340 kg CO₂/t) is NOT harmonised and has no OJ citation. These most climate-relevant cements are excluded from CE marking and DPP. Only path: prEN 197-1 revision incorporating CEM VI.

content.about: Covers system chimneys and their components, including metal, clay/ceramic and concrete chimney liners, flue pipes, flue blocks, outer wall elements, flue terminals, chimney fans, draught regulators, air/flue terminal systems for room-sealed appliances, and chimney kits (via EADs). Materials span metal, clay/ceramic, concrete and plastic. Free-standing industrial chimneys are excluded and follow a separate standardisation track. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 166 (hENs) and EOTA (EADs).
content.standards_landscape: 18 harmonised product standards are cited in the OJ under mandate M/105 (CPR 305/2011), with AVCP System 2+ as the primary system for general characteristics (reaction to fire assessed separately under System 1 or 3 per Commission Decision 95/467/EC as amended by 2010/679/EU). Key standards include: EN 1856-1:2009 and EN 1856-2:2009 (metal chimneys), EN 1857:2010 and EN 1858:2008+A1:2011 (concrete components), EN 1457-1:2012 and EN 1457-2:2012 (clay/ceramic flue liners), EN 13063-1/2/3 (clay/ceramic system chimneys), EN 14471:2013+A1:2015 (plastic flue liners), and EN 14989-1/2:2007 (room-sealed applications). EN 1443:2019 serves as the general requirements reference standard. 11 chimney-specific EADs exist under Product Area 06, of which 10 are cited in the OJ (7 via Implementing Decision (EU) 2019/450 as amended, 3 via earlier C-series OJ communications). All under AVCP System 1. EAD 060017 is pending OJ citation. These are old-regime EADs under CPR 305/2011 — they can be used for issuing ETAs until 9 January 2031, and existing ETAs remain valid for CE marking until 9 January 2036. DPP does not apply to products covered by these old-regime EADs.
content.standards_development: CEN/TC 166 is proactively revising several standards under its own work programme. These revisions are TC-driven under the existing mandate M/105 (CPR 305/2011) — they are not yet mandated under CPR 2024/3110 and do not trigger DPP obligations on their own. However, the revision work is expected to be incorporated into the forthcoming standardisation request under CPR 2024/3110.• prEN 1856-1 rev — metal system chimney products (revision status uncertain — genorma shows CEN project deleted Mar 2021, but DIN/BSI references suggest a draft was circulated and may have restarted)• prEN 1856-2 rev — metal flue liners and connecting flue pipes (under development)• prEN 13384-2 rev — thermal and fluid dynamic calculation methods Part 2 (stage 40.10, target Jan 2026)
content.sreq_analysis: Not yet adopted. No Art. 12 notification filed. Per COM(2025) 772 (First CPR Working Plan 2026–2029), a standardisation request covering most chimney and flue products is planned for adoption in Q3 2026. Self-standing chimneys (EN 13084 series) will be covered by a separate standardisation request for facade fire testing standards, planned for 2027. The CPR acquis sub-group for chimneys has not yet formally started but is expected to complete work in 2026. The future SReq will be issued under CPR 2024/3110 (new regulation), replacing the old mandate M/105.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2031–2032, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted Q3 2026 and delivery expected 2028, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2031–2032. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 18 hENs and 11 EADs range from ~2031 to ~2032 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). Both scenarios converge on the same timeline.
content.stakeholder_notes: Structural demand shift: heat pump sales overtook gas boiler sales in Germany in 2025, reducing gas flue demand. Biomass heating and solid fuel appliances maintain chimney relevance. Industry associations: ECA (European Chimney Association), ESCHFOE.
content.key_risks: (1) prEN 1856-1 revision uncertainty — genorma shows CEN project deleted Mar 2021, but DIN/BSI references suggest draft may have restarted. Flagship metal chimney standard. (2) SReq not yet started — planned Q3 2026 but dependent on Milestone III completion. (3) 11 legacy EADs expire 9 Jan 2031 — notes indicate 'no new EAD expected under CPR 2024/3110', implying transition to hEN route only. (4) Self-standing chimneys (EN 13084) excluded from CHI SReq — separate timeline creates coordination risk. (5) Very low EPD readiness: only Schiedel has an EN 15804 chimney EPD. Industry must build environmental data infrastructure largely from scratch for DPP. (6) No hydrogen-ready chimney standard exists. As hydrogen blending in gas networks increases (EU Hydrogen Strategy), chimney products may face new performance requirements not covered by current hENs. CEN/TC 166 monitoring needed.
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 CPR Working Plan 2026–2029 [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; nlfnorm.cz hEN database [S143]; EOTA EAD database [S144]; CEN/TC 166 project listings.

content.about: Covers products used in road, airfield, and trafficked-area construction, including asphalt and bituminous mixtures, paving-grade and polymer-modified bitumen, slurry surfacing, surface dressing, cold-applied joint sealants, road marking materials, concrete pavements, and hydraulically bound and unbound mixtures. The product scope is large, with around 25 harmonised standards. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 227, with CEN/TC 336 (bituminous binders) and CEN/TC 226 (road equipment) contributing related standards.
content.standards_landscape: ~25 harmonised standards cited in OJ under CPR 305/2011. AVCP per Commission Decision 98/601/EC (13 Oct 1998). Key series: EN 13108-1 to -9 (bituminous mixture specs), EN 13108-20/-21 (type testing/FPC), EN 12591 (paving bitumen), EN 14023 (polymer modified bitumen), EN 12273 (slurry surfacing), EN 12274 (surface dressing), EN 14188 (joint sealants), EN 1436/EN 1871 (road markings), EN 13877 (concrete pavements), EN 14227 (hydraulically bound mixtures), EN 13285 (unbound mixtures).
content.standards_development: No CPR-mandated standard revisions currently under way. The CPR acquis process has not yet started for this family, so no standardisation-request-driven revisions are in the CEN/TC 227 pipeline.
content.sreq_analysis: Not adopted. No draft on CIRCABC. Per COM(2025) 772 (First CPR Working Plan 2026–2029), the acquis process targets: Milestone I (scope) Q1 2026, Milestone III (essential characteristics) Q3 2026, standardisation request 2027. The future SReq will be under CPR 2024/3110 (new regulation). Road construction is in the later priority group), with acquis work not yet started.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2032–2033, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted 2027, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2032–2033. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 28 hENs range from ~2032 to ~2033 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). Both scenarios converge on the same timeline.
content.stakeholder_notes: EAPA (asphalt producers) and Eurobitume (bitumen producers) coordinate as joint industry voice. EAPA 'Asphalt 4.0' digital initiative explores BIM integration and data-driven quality management — directly relevant to DPP infrastructure. Eurobitume LCA 4.0 (Mar 2025) revised bitumen GWP from 216 to 530 kgCO₂e/t (+145%). EU-27: 208.5 Mt asphalt production (2024), ~$20.5B bitumen market. One of highest-tonnage CPR families.
content.key_risks: (1) Eurobitume LCA 4.0 GWP revision: bitumen GWP100 revised from 216 to 530 kgCO₂e/t (+145%, Mar 2025). All existing EPDs understated — DPP declarations must use updated figure. (2) EN 13043/13055 AVCP mismatch with AGG ('2+' vs '2+, 4'). (3) RAP recycling: 45.5 Mt/yr available, 76% reused — DPP recycled content data directly available but methodology varies by Member State.
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; nlfnorm.cz [S143]; EOTA [S144]; EAPA statistics 2024; Eurobitume LCA 4.0 (Mar 2025).

content.about: One of the broadest product families, covering all types of floor coverings and paving used in buildings and external works. This includes resilient products (vinyl, linoleum, rubber, cork), textile and laminate floor coverings, wood flooring, ceramic tiles, natural stone tiles and slabs, concrete paving blocks, clay pavers, terrazzo, floor screeds, raised access floors, and indoor sports surfaces. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 134, with contributions from several related committees including TC 67 (ceramic tiles), TC 178 (paving units), TC 246 (natural stone), and TC 303 (floor screeds).
content.standards_landscape: ~19 hENs cited under CPR 305/2011 (OJ C 092/2018 and Implementing Decision (EU) 2019/451). Key standards: EN 14041:2004/AC:2006 (resilient, textile and laminate — essential characteristics; EN 14041:2018 published by CEN but OJ citation unclear — likely standardisation crisis gap), EN 14342:2013 (wood flooring), EN 14411:2016 (ceramic tiles), EN 13813:2002 (screeds). AVCP Systems 1 (reaction to fire), 3 (slip resistance, formaldehyde, PCP), and 4 (other characteristics) per Commission Decision 97/808/EC. Plus EADs under EU 2019/450 with amendments through EU 2025/1769.
content.standards_development: EPLF (European Producers of Laminate Flooring) working on EN 14041 revision to integrate hazardous substances and environmental aspects into CE labelling. FEP (European Federation of Parquet Industry) active in adapting wood flooring standards to revised CPR. Milestone 0 work already ongoing on test methods for slip resistance — EN 16165:2021 (CEN/TC 339) published, Commission developing assessment method before formal acquis.
content.sreq_analysis: Not yet adopted. No acquis sub-group formally established. Per COM(2025) 772 Table 3: Milestone I targeted Q2 2026, Milestone III targeted Q4 2026, SReq targeted 2027. No standards delivery or mandatory dates set.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2031–2032, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted 2027, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2031–2032. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 19 hENs range from ~2031 to ~2032 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). Both scenarios converge on the same timeline.
content.stakeholder_notes: Most varied DPP readiness landscape of any CPR family. Ceramics: Confindustria Ceramica industry EPD covers 82.6% of Italian production. Laminate: EPLF EPDs since 2009. Natural stone: EUROROC promotes EPDs. Vinyl: VOC/chemical data focus dominates over full EN 15804 EPDs. Wood: linked to STP family. Screed: minimal EPDs.
content.key_risks: (1) 8-TC coordination: CEN/TC 134 (resilient), TC 178 (paving), TC 128 (tiles), TC 303 (floor screeds), TC 134 WG 7 (laminate), etc. Most complex TC landscape. (2) Vinyl compound regulatory burden: REACH + CPR dangerous substances (CEN/TC 351) + national VOC requirements. (3) $52–62B market with 6+ material types — DPP implementation speeds will vary by material.
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; nlfnorm.cz [S143]; EOTA [S144]; EPLF laminate EPDs; Confindustria Ceramica industry EPD; EUROROC.

standards_summary.source: COM(2025) 772 + EOTA PA 34. EAD-only family — kits and systems.

content.about: Covers an exceptionally diverse range of construction kits and prefabricated elements, from timber building kits and prefabricated building units to structural sandwich panels, earthquake-resistant steel-concrete kits, falling rock protection systems, and fibre-reinforced polymer strengthening kits. This family also includes family 36 (attached ladders) under the CPR Working Plan. Uniquely, products are assessed exclusively through European Assessment Documents and European Technical Assessments, with no harmonised EN standards. Standardisation is managed by EOTA, with around 32 active EADs in this product area.
content.standards_landscape: ~32 active EADs cited in OJ under Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2019/450 with amendments through (EU) 2025/1769 (3 Sep 2025). 66 new EADs published across all product areas in Nov 2025 + 9 in Jan 2026. EOTA Stakeholder Conference (5 Nov 2025): "From Vision to Action: The New ETA Route" discussed the transition framework. EADs under outgoing CPR can be used for issuing ETAs until 9 January 2031 (five years from entry into force). ETAs issued before that date remain valid for CE marking until 9 January 2036 (ten years from entry into force). New EADs must include environmental sustainability characteristics: GWP declaration immediately, core LCA-EPD indicators per EN 15804 by 2029, full indicator list by 2031. EADs now have 10-year validity with extension option. ETAs under the new CPR have no expiry date. Multiple entities (groups, associations, Commission) may now request ETAs — not just individual manufacturers. EAD 340033 (prefab chimney kits with flue liner) overlaps with the CHI family — cross-family data consistency should be verified. COM(2025) 772 lists PA 36 (attached ladders) under KAS — potential overlap with LAD family. Verify scope boundaries when SReq defined.
content.standards_development: CEN/TC 88 (thermal insulation) and multiple component TCs involved. Kit standards require coordination across insulation, adhesive, mesh, render, and fixing standards — no single TC controls the full kit scope.
content.sreq_analysis: Not yet adopted. No acquis sub-group formally established. Per COM(2025) 772 Table 3: Milestone I targeted Q2 2026, Milestone III targeted Q2 2027, SReq targeted 2027. No standards delivery or mandatory dates set. May be divided into several workstreams given the diversity of products. Commission may request EADs for innovative kits.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2032, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted 2027, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2032. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 32 EADs converge on ~2032. Both scenarios converge on the same timeline.
content.stakeholder_notes: ETICS market €7.7B (2023) → €12.2B by 2031 (CAGR 5.9%). Prefab market €78B (2025) → €109.3B by 2030 (CAGR 7%). Combined KAS among the largest CPR families by market value. Renovation Wave is a major demand driver for ETICS.
content.key_risks: (1) Kit/system DPP compound architecture: 3-layer data challenge (component → system → building level). EC feasibility study has limited kit-specific guidance. Most complex DPP challenge of any CPR family. (2) ETICS EPD gap: only ~15 ETICS EPDs vs 798 ETAs issued (50:1 gap). Kit LCA complexity (insulation + adhesive + mesh + render + fixings) makes EPD system boundaries particularly challenging. (3) EAD transition scale: 36 EADs expire 9 Jan 2031 — among the largest EAD portfolios requiring transition.
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 CPR Working Plan 2026–2029 [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; EOTA EAD database [S144]; EAE (European Association for ETICS). EC DPP feasibility study.

content.about: This is a new product family introduced in CPR 2024/3110, covering permanently attached ladders fixed to buildings and other construction works. This includes fixed roof ladders, vertical access ladders on facades and building exteriors, caged and uncaged maintenance access ladders, and fixed ladders in utility shafts. Related roof access products such as walkways, treads and safety hooks are also in scope. Portable ladders, loft ladders, and machinery access ladders are excluded. No dedicated technical committee has been assigned; existing standards are under CEN/TC 128 (roof covering products).
content.standards_landscape: Three standards currently cited under CPR 305/2011 in product family 22 (Roof Coverings) via Implementing Decision (EU) 2019/451 — all AVCP System 3: (1) EN 12951:2004 — permanently fixed roof ladders on pitched roofs (closest existing standard to family 36 scope); (2) EN 516:2006 — roof access walkways, treads and steps; (3) EN 517:2006 — roof safety hooks and anchorage points. Under CPR 2024/3110, these may be reclassified from family 22 to family 36. Related standards under other legislation: EN ISO 14122-4:2016 (fixed ladders for machinery — Machinery Regulation), EN 353-1:2014+A1:2017 (guided fall arresters on fixed ladders — PPE Regulation).
content.standards_development: No active prEN registrations. EN 12951:2004, EN 516:2006, EN 517:2006 are 18–22 years old and require significant revision. No dedicated TC — uses CEN/TC 128 (ROC), creating potential resource contention.
content.sreq_analysis: Not yet adopted. No independent milestone data in Table 3 — family 36 is handled within family 34 (KAS) per COM(2025) 772. KAS milestones: M-I Q2 2026, M-III Q2 2027, SReq 2027. Table 3 notes "may be divided in several workstreams" — ladders could follow a separate timeline from KAS's main EOTA-based track.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2031–2032, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: Based on the Commission Working Plan, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2031–2032. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 3 hENs range from ~2031 to ~2032 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). Both scenarios converge on the same timeline. Zero EPDs for any LAD product. Metal sector EPD inheritance (from structural steel/aluminium EPDs) is a potential pathway for DPP environmental data.
content.stakeholder_notes: Standards gap: EN 12951 covers only roof ladders on pitched roofs. Family 36's broader scope (facade ladders, utility access ladders, caged ladders) has no existing harmonised standards. New standards will need to be developed — likely via CEN mandate rather than EOTA EAD route, creating a potentially different pathway from the rest of family 34 (KAS). Niche market segment. ZARGES is European market leader. EU Directive 2001/45/EC positions ladders as 'last resort' for working at height.
content.key_risks: (1) Standards gap: EN 12951 covers only roof ladders on pitched roofs. Family 36’s broader scope (facade ladders, utility access, caged ladders) has no existing hENs. (2) No dedicated TC — uses CEN/TC 128 (ROC), potential resource contention. (3) Family 36 handled within KAS (family 34) — may be delayed by more complex KAS product categories (ETICS, prefab). (4) EN 12951, EN 516, EN 517 are 18–22 years old. (5) Three-regulation overlap: CPR (EN 12951/516/517) + Machinery Regulation (EN 14122-4) + PPE (EN 353-1) for fixed ladders. Regulatory boundary unclear.

standards_summary.source: COM(2025) 772 + TC 459/SC 4 work programme. 0 current hENs (EN 10080 withdrawn). 2 prENs in development. 2 EADs for PT kits.

content.about: Covers three distinct product groups: reinforcing steel (weldable bars, coils, and welded fabric for concrete reinforcement), prestressing steel (wire, strand, and bars), and post-tensioning kits (complete systems with tensile elements, anchorages, ducts, and filling material). Also includes stainless steel reinforcement. This is the only product family where the main harmonised standard was withdrawn, meaning reinforcing steel currently cannot be CE marked in the EU. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 459/SC 4.
content.standards_landscape: This is the only product family where the main harmonised standard was withdrawn from the OJ. EN 10080:2005 was withdrawn by Commission Decision 2006/893/EC because its "framework" approach left technical classes to national standards, making CE marking impossible. Since 2008, reinforcing steel cannot be CE marked in the EU. Prestressing steel (EN 10138 series) has been stuck at prEN stage for 20+ years and was never cited in the OJ. Only post-tensioning kits have a functioning CE marking route via ETAs under EAD 160004-00-0301 and EAD 160027-00-0301 (cited OJ C 435/07, 15 Dec 2017), AVCP System 1+. Major ETA holders include VSL, Freyssinet, DYWIDAG, OVM, and MK4.
content.standards_development: prEN 10080:2023 — comprehensive revision (183 pages) went to CEN Enquiry in early 2023. Status post-enquiry unclear but likely being coordinated with the new SReq. prEN 10138-3:2023 (strand) at stage 40.60 (close of voting, Aug 2023). prEN 10138-2 (wire) at stage 20.60. prEN 10370 (stainless steel reinforcement) under development. Second-generation Eurocode 2 (EN 1992-1-1:2023) extends steel grades to B700 and adds stainless reinforcement — references EN 10080 and prEN 10138 despite neither functioning as a harmonised standard.
content.sreq_analysis: Currently under preparation per COM(2025) 772 (one of 4 SReqs being prepared). CEN WP 2025 confirms CEN/TC 459/SC 4 "will participate in the consultation process around the draft Standardization Request." Old mandate M/115 (1997, revised 2009) is dormant. Assigned placeholder "M/XXX" — no mandate number yet. Target adoption Q2 2026. Delivery 2029, standard mandatory + DA 2029. Product requirements delegated act under discussion. CPR Acquis sub-group established Q4 2022 (M-I finished, M-III targeted Q4 2025). AVCP System 1+ (Decision 97/597/EC). European Ombudsman case opened Jan 2019 on Commission's failure to follow up on M/115.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2030–2032, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted Q2 2026 and delivery expected 2029, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2030–2031. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 2 hENs and 2 EADs range from ~2030 to ~2032. The combined range spans from the standards-based assessment’s earlier estimate (~2030–2032) to the policy pipeline’s later horizon (~2030–2031).
content.stakeholder_notes: CEN/TC 459/SC 4 feeds into acquis subgroup. Eurocode 2 (EN 1992-1-1:2023) published Nov 2023, normatively references EN 10080 and prEN 10138 despite neither being harmonised — design code/product standard gap persists until ~2028. SteelZero (40+ organisations) commits to 50% lower-emission steel by 2030, creating demand-side GWP transparency pressure. ResponsibleSteel certification. ArcelorMittal XCarb (300 kg CO₂e/t), Feralpi FERGreen (368 kg CO₂e/t), SERFAS all have EN 15804+A2 EPDs — data infrastructure exists; EN 10080 harmonisation is the sole blocker.
content.key_risks: (1) 20-year CE marking void: EN 10080:2005 withdrawn 2006, no replacement harmonised. Only CPR family where CE marking is impossible. (2) prEN 10080 at stage 40.60 with no advancement found. (3) EU Ombudsman case 2145/2018/JAP (opened Jan 2019) — status unresolved. (4) ESPR iron & steel delegated act (~2027 compliance) may give RPS an ESPR DPP before its CPR DPP — dual regulatory pathway unique to this family. (5) CBAM full financial implementation Jan 2026 — triple-compliance burden (CBAM + CPR DPP + ESPR DPP). (6) Pipeline A NT-3 (Milestone III) targeted Q4 2025, still shows not_started as of Mar 2026 — likely stale. Verify against Commission/CEN sources.
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 CPR Working Plan [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; Concrete Centre (CE marking impossibility reference); EU Ombudsman case 2145/2018/JAP; EUROFER ESPR position; ArcelorMittal/Feralpi/SERFAS EPD sources.

standards_summary.source: Draft SReq (Jan 2026) + OJ C 092/2018 + EOTA PA 2. 15 hENs + 7 EADs.

content.about: One of the broadest and most commercially significant product families, covering windows, external and internal pedestrian doorsets, industrial and garage doors, fire and smoke control doorsets, powered pedestrian doors, external blinds, awnings, shutters, roof windows, and hatches. It also includes building hardware such as door closers, hold-open devices, emergency and panic exit devices, hinges, and locks. With around 50,000 manufacturers and an estimated 76 million DPPs per year for windows alone, this family represents a major implementation challenge. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 33.
content.standards_landscape: EN 14351-1:2006+A2:2016 (windows/external doors, AVCP System 1 for fire / System 3 for thermal/acoustic), EN 13241:2003+A2:2016 (industrial/commercial/garage doors, also under Machinery Directive), EN 16034:2014 (fire/smoke — must be used with EN 14351-1 or EN 13241), EN 13561:2015 and EN 13659:2015 (blinds/shutters, System 4), and 8 building hardware standards (EN 1154, EN 1155, EN 1158, EN 179, EN 1125, EN 1935, EN 12209, EN 14846 — all System 1 for escape/fire, System 3 otherwise). All cited via OJ C 092/6 and Implementing Decision (EU) 2019/451 under old CPR 305/2011 mandate M/101. EN 14351-2 (internal doors) published 2018 but NEVER cited — victim of the post-James Elliott standardisation deficit where the Commission froze new hEN citations.
content.standards_development: The draft SReq (21 Jan 2026) requests 15 deliverables: 4 new product standards (windows/hatches/doorsets, industrial/commercial/garage doors, rooflights/hatches, building hardware — all due 15 Jun 2029), 5 new EPD product category rules (due Jun–Dec 2028), and 6 revisions (EN 13241 gates/barriers due Jun 2029; EN 13561 awnings, EN 13561 external blinds, EN 13120 internal blinds, EN 13659 shutters — all due 15 Dec 2029; EN 17610 EPD PCR building hardware due Dec 2028). prEN 14351-3 also exists at CEN — would merge EN 16034's fire/smoke approach into the EN 14351 series. Dangerous substances assessment will follow the horizontal SReq for dangerous substances (1 Sep 2025, under CPR 2024/3110, 15 deliverables by CEN/TC 351, all due 31 Dec 2029 — covers PFAS, microplastics, VVOC, asbestos, radioactivity). Under the new SReq, the EN 14351 series is being reorganised into 4 consolidated standards: (1) Pedestrian doorsets = EN 14351-1 (excl. windows) + EN 14351-2 + EN 16034; (2) Windows = EN 14351-1 (excl. doors) + EN 16034; (3) Fire industrial doors = EN 13241 + EN 16034; (4) Non-fire industrial doors = EN 13241. This restructuring will significantly change the current 15-hEN listing.
content.sreq_analysis: Draft Art. 12 notification published 21 Jan 2026 via the EC Notification System. Feedback deadline was 18 Feb 2026. NOT yet adopted — EuroWindoor submitted comments 13 Feb 2026. Under CPR 2024/3110. Replaces mandate M/101 (expires 31 Dec 2026 per Article 5). Decision expires 30 Jun 2031. First annual report due 30 Jun 2027. Final report due 30 Jun 2030. Scope: 51 technical characteristics + 19 environmental criteria + 257 substances for windows alone. Industry pushback: 86 comments on 4th draft (Oct 2025), EuroWindoor calls timeline "highly ambitious", wants 1-year extension. AVCP System 1 for fire/escape; System 3 otherwise (Decision 1999/93/EC, amended 2011/246/EU). Joint AVS 4+ proposal (Feb 2026) for verified software assessment. Product requirements DA for escape routes targeted 2029. Acquis sub-group established Q4 2022 (M-I and M-III both finished).
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2030–2032, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request draft published and delivery expected Jun 2029, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2030–2031. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 15 hENs and 7 EADs range from ~2030 to ~2032 (13 of 15 hENs under CPR 305/2011, needing revision under CPR 2024/3110). The combined range spans from the standards-based assessment’s earlier estimate (~2030–2032) to the policy pipeline’s later horizon (~2030–2031).
content.stakeholder_notes: EuroWindoor submitted 86 comments on 4th draft SReq (Oct 2025) and further comments on revised draft (13 Feb 2026). Industry calls Jun 2029 delivery timeline 'highly ambitious' and requests 1-year extension. Joint AVS 4+ proposal (Feb 2026) for verified software assessment submitted. AISBL technical workshop on CPR 2024/3110 implementation ongoing. PVC recycling: VinylPlus targets 1 Mt/year by 2030 (2024 actual: 724,638t). EPPA 5-year action plan boosting PVC window recycling in France, Germany, Poland (pilot evaluation 2026). Aluminium windows infinitely recyclable. Wood windows have biogenic carbon accounting considerations (EN 15804+A2). DPP under new CPR requires end-of-life/recyclability data — existing recycling infrastructure provides data foundation.
content.key_risks: (1) Timeline risk: 15 deliverables in ~3 years is aggressive for CEN/TC 33. (2) Dual regulation: EN 13241 (industrial doors) also under Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC — coordination needed. (3) DPP volume: ~76 million DPPs/year for windows alone — infrastructure scaling challenge. (4) Standardisation deficit: EN 14351-2 (internal doors) never cited since 2018 — risk of continued delay. (5) AVS 4+ proposal: EuroWindoor, European Aluminium, EPPA, Glass for Europe, and SBS jointly proposed a new Assessment and Verification System (Feb 2026) where a Notified Body verifies input data and validates software tools. If adopted via delegated act under Art. 10(4), this fundamentally changes the verification regime. (6) EuroWindoor warns standardisation work ‘risks failing to deliver the required quality within the set timeframe’ with key templates and guidance documents still missing. No prEN 14351-1 (new), prEN 14351-2, or prEN 14351-3 registrations found. (7) SReq scope complexity: Draft lists 51 technical characteristics, 19 environmental criteria, and 257 substances for windows — industry argues excessive. May be reduced during CEN acceptance negotiation. (8) EPBD dual GWP demand: EPBD recast requires building-level GWP calculations (large new >2000m² from 2027, all new from 2030). Glass industry EPD infrastructure is mature (AGC, Guardian), but window assembly-level data is the bottleneck. Creates urgency for GWP data preparation before new CPR standards are published.
content.sources_summary: CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; EC Notification System Art. 12 (DWS SReq draft, Jan 2026); EuroWindoor publications and SReq comments (Feb 2026); ift Rosenheim CPR articles; VinylPlus Progress Report 2025; EPPA circular economy programme; Directive (EU) 2024/1275 (EPBD recast).

standards_summary.source: COM(2025) 772 + CEN/TC 88 + DIBt hEN list + EOTA PA 4. 12 hENs + 18 EADs.

content.about: The largest insulation product family under the CPR, covering factory-made thermal insulation products across 13 material types including mineral wool, expanded and extruded polystyrene, rigid polyurethane, phenolic foam, cellular glass, wood wool, wood fibre, expanded cork, expanded perlite, polyethylene, and calcium silicate. Also includes external thermal insulation composite systems (ETICS) assessed via the EAD route, vacuum insulation panels, and reflective insulation. This is a substantial market, valued at approximately USD 21 billion globally. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 88.
content.standards_landscape: 12 hENs for factory-made products: EN 13162–13171 series (one per insulation type) plus EN 14063-1, EN 16069, and EN 16977. All cited under old CPR 305/2011 via mandate M/103 (last OJ citation: OJ C 092, 2018 / Implementing Decision 2019/451). Important correction: EN 13499 and EN 13500 (ETICS with EPS/mineral wool) are not cited in the Official Journal and never functioned as true harmonised standards — ETICS products use the EAD/ETA route instead. AVCP: System 3/4 for factory-made products (Decision 99/91/EC); System 1 for ETICS (Decision 97/556/EC, amended by 2011/14/EU). EAD 042461-00-1201 (hydrophilic mineral wool for green roofs, published May 2025, cited via Implementing Decision 2025/871) is not yet in TIP standards[]. If confirmed in PA 04, ead_count increases from 18 to 19.
content.sreq_analysis: In preparation — targeted Q3 2026 per COM(2025) 772 Table 3. Not yet notified under Art. 12 (as of Feb 2026). Acquis Milestone I (scope definition) finished; Milestone III (technical content for SReq + delegated acts) ongoing. Product requirements delegated act under discussion. The SReq will be under CPR 2024/3110 (new CPR), meaning DPP obligations apply directly once the resulting HTS is in force. Separately, the horizontal dangerous substances SReq (CEN/TC 351) was notified 1 Sep 2025 and covers all families including TIP — key substances: HBCDD, formaldehyde, biocides, mineral fibres. M/103 (mid-1990s) will be superseded by the new SReq.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2029–2032, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted Q3 2026 and delivery expected 2029, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2029–2030. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 12 hENs and 18 EADs range from ~2031 to ~2032 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). The combined range spans from the policy pipeline’s earlier estimate (~2029–2030) to the standards-based assessment’s later horizon (~2031–2032). Knauf Insulation, Rockwool, and EUMEPS all publish EN 15804+A2 EPDs. EUMEPS sector EPD model (2024) enables SME EPS producers. Strong mineral wool and EPS EPD infrastructure.
content.stakeholder_notes: ETICS dual pathway: ETICS kits currently rely exclusively on the EAD/ETA route. ETAG 004 was fully converted to EAD 040083-01-0404 in 2025, with approximately 798 valid ETAs. A future harmonised product standard — EN 17237 — is in development by CEN/TC 88/WG 18 under mandate M/489, but is estimated to be 7+ years from completion. No harmonised facade fire test exists at EU level — national divergence persists (BS 8414 UK, DIN 4102-20 DE, LEPIR 2 FR). The European Association for ETICS (EAE) represents >80% of Europe's ETICS revenue. Around 15–20 active EADs cover innovative products including VIPs, aerogels, and bio-based insulation. Environmental & sustainability: EN 16783:2024 (EPD Product Category Rules for insulation) published. GWP declaration becomes mandatory from January 2026 under CPR 2024/3110. AVS delegated act circulated to Member States July 2025. ESPR: thermal insulation is not on the first priority product list (2025–2030 working plan) — CPR 2024/3110 is the primary instrument for environmental performance requirements. No dual ESPR/CPR DPP obligation expected in the near term (ESPR mid-term review in 2028 could add construction products). European insulation market $21B (2024), CAGR 6.32% to $36.5B by 2033. EPBD renovation wave targets 35M buildings by 2030. TIP is among the largest CPR families by market value.
content.key_risks: (1) ETICS transition: ~798 ETAs under EAD 040083 — massive transition burden if EAD route closes before hEN alternative is ready. (2) hEN count discrepancy: content references 13 but data has 12 — resolved to 12. (3) New EAD 042461 (green roof mineral wool) may expand portfolio. (4) Energy efficiency coupling: thermal insulation performance directly impacts building energy requirements under EPBD — regulatory interdependency. (5) EPS ETICS waste expected to triple by 2050 (up to €400/t disposal). France EPR for building materials and Austria 2027 landfill ban add regulatory urgency.
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 CPR Working Plan [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; nlfnorm.cz hEN database [S143]; EOTA EAD database [S144]; DIBt hEN list; Implementing Decision 2025/871.

standards_summary.source: COM(2025) 772 + CEN/TC 129 + EOTA PA 30. 12 hENs + 4 EADs.

content.about: Covers all types of flat glass, profiled glass, and glass blocks used in buildings — from basic float glass and toughened safety glass to laminated glass, insulating glass units, coated glass, and specialty products like borosilicate and chemically strengthened glass. Six European Assessment Documents also cover niche innovations such as vacuum-insulated glass units and thermally curved glass. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 129 (Glass in building), which coordinates closely with Glass for Europe.
content.standards_landscape: ~12 standard families cited under old CPR 305/2011 via mandate M/135 (OJ C 092/2018 and Implementing Decision (EU) 2019/451). Key hENs: EN 572 series (basic glass), EN 1096 series (coated glass), EN 1279 series (insulating glass units), EN 12150-2 (toughened safety glass), EN 1863-2 (heat-strengthened), EN 14449 (laminated glass), EN 1051-2 (glass blocks), EN 1748-1-2/1748-2-2 (borosilicate/glass-ceramic), EN 14178-2 (alkaline earth silicate), EN 12337-2 (chemically strengthened), EN 14179-2 (heat-soaked toughened). Implementing Decision 2025/871 (30 Apr 2025) cited new EAD(s) for PET foils in insulating glass units. Specific EAD number pending identification — may expand GLA's EAD portfolio.
content.standards_development: SReq targeted Q1 2026 per COM(2025) 772 Table 3. As of 1 Mar 2026, no Art. 12 notification or formal adoption confirmed. Scale: 30+ standards under CEN/TC 129 require revision. Monitor weekly for SReq publication.
content.sreq_analysis: Acquis Milestone I (product scope) finished; Milestone III (essential characteristics) ongoing. SReq GLA targeted Q1 2026 per COM(2025) 772 Table 3. Under CPR 2024/3110 (DPP applies directly). No Art. 12 notification found as of Feb 2026 — Q1 2026 target at risk. Glass for Europe confirms 30+ product standards to be written or revised. SReq aligned with windows/doors SReq timeline (CEN/TC 33). Product requirements DA (Article 7 — minimum performance thresholds) identified for 2028. IGU alignment gap: window hEN target Dec 2028 but CEN/TC 129 IGU standards not until Dec 2030 — 2-year component mismatch flagged by industry.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2029–2032, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted Q1 2026 and delivery expected 2028, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2029–2030. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 12 hENs and 4 EADs range from ~2029 to ~2032 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). The combined range spans from the standards-based assessment’s earlier estimate (~2029–2032) to the policy pipeline’s later horizon (~2029–2030). AGC EPD Generator covers 30+ environmental indicators (EN 15804+A2, third-party verified). Guardian, Glas Trösch, and Tvitec also publish EPDs. GLA is among the most DPP-ready families.
content.stakeholder_notes: AVCP system: Commission Decision 2000/245/EC — System 1 (fire resistance, anti-bullet, anti-explosion glass); System 3 (other safety glass — toughened, laminated without fire/security function); System 4 (regular glass products). Basic glass classified A1 (non-combustible) without testing per Decision 96/603/EC. CEN/TC 129 recommends NPD for reaction to fire of laminated glass and IGUs (SBI test not appropriate for glass products). Environmental & sustainability: EN 17074:2019 is the dedicated EPD PCR for flat glass (cradle to gate/grave). Glass for Europe opposes worst-case approach for environmental declarations — complex multi-site value chain makes this impractical. EPBD guidance (June 2025) pushes U-value + g-value for transparent building elements. Only 5% of construction flat glass is recycled; industry calls for 15% minimum recycled content target. ESPR: glass NOT on 2025-2030 priority list. Also affected by horizontal dangerous substances SReq (CEN/TC 351, all due 31 Dec 2029).
content.key_risks: (1) SReq Q1 2026 target approaching — no public notification found yet. (2) Scale of standards revision: 30+ standards need revision by CEN/TC 129. (3) New EAD citation (2025/871) for PET foils in insulating glass — may expand EAD portfolio. (4) Energy efficiency: glass performance directly impacts building energy declarations — tight coupling with EPBD requirements. (5) Only 5% of construction & demolition flat glass is recycled; 80%+ ends in landfill. DPP recycled content data infrastructure is underdeveloped. (6) EPBD guidance (June 2025) now encourages both U-value AND g-value for transparent elements. DPP data must carry both thermal and solar performance aligned with EPBD.
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 CPR Working Plan 2026–2029 [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; nlfnorm.cz hEN database [S143]; EOTA EAD database [S144]; Glass for Europe CPR implementation page.

standards_summary.source: COM(2025) 772 + CEN/TC 124 + CEN/TC 112 + EOTA PA 13.

content.about: Covers structural timber products including strength-graded solid timber, glued laminated timber (glulam), cross-laminated timber (CLT), laminated veneer lumber (LVL), finger-jointed timber, prefabricated trusses, wood poles, and timber fasteners and connectors. Around 41 European Assessment Documents also cover innovative products such as hardwood glulam, wood-concrete composites, and laminated strand lumber. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 124 (Timber structures), with key industry bodies including CEI-Bois and EOS.
content.standards_landscape: ~9 harmonised standards cited under old CPR 305/2011 via mandate M/112 (OJ C 092/2018 and Implementing Decision (EU) 2019/451). Key hENs: EN 14081-1:2016+A1:2019 (strength graded timber — System 2+), EN 14080:2013 (glulam — System 1), EN 16351:2021 (CLT — System 1), EN 14374:2004 (LVL — System 1), EN 15497:2014 (finger-jointed — System 1), EN 14250:2010 (prefab trusses — System 1/2+), EN 14229:2010 (wood poles — System 2+), EN 14545:2008 (connectors — System 2+/3), EN 14592:2022 (dowel fasteners — System 3). EN 16351 is a key success story — CLT previously relied on EAD 130005-00-0304; harmonised standard published 2015, revised 2021. Delegated Regulations (EU) 2017/2293 and (EU) 2017/1227 provide reaction-to-fire classification without testing for CLT, LVL, glulam, and finger-jointed timber. EN 13986 (wood-based panels for construction) is cross-listed with the WBP family. Both families track identical data: AVCP System 1/2+/3/4, CEN/TC 112, CPR 2011 regime.
content.standards_development: EN 1995-1-1:2025 (Eurocode 5, second generation) published Aug 2025. Substantially expanded to include CLT, LVL, and glulam design rules. NSB national annexes in progress — will directly inform the SReq essential characteristics scope for STP.
content.sreq_analysis: SReq STP targeted Q3 2026 per COM(2025) 772 Table 3. Under CPR 2024/3110 (DPP applies directly). No Art. 12 notification found as of Feb 2026 — consistent with Q3 2026 target. Will require revision of all ~9 existing hENs plus new standards. Milestone I ongoing, Milestone III targeted Q1 2026. Also affected by horizontal dangerous substances SReq (CEN/TC 351, notified 1 Sep 2025 — timber-specific concerns include preservatives, formaldehyde, biocides, VOCs).
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2029–2033, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted Q3 2026 and delivery expected 2029, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2029–2030. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 9 hENs and 41 EADs range from ~2032 to ~2033 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). The combined range spans from the policy pipeline’s earlier estimate (~2029–2030) to the standards-based assessment’s later horizon (~2032–2033). STP is priority 7 of 34 product families per COM(2025) 772, but fast-track standards updating is NOT available (confirmed by Innovawood/EOS). Must go through full acquis→SReq→CEN→OJ pipeline. CEI-Bois TIMBIM initiative: 12 data templates on 7 hENs published in bSDD since 2023. Templates cover product properties, GS1 identifiers (GTIN, GLN, GMN), EPD data, and circularity data. GS1 identifiers align with CPR DPP unique product identifier requirements. Industry DPP groundwork underway but manufacturer adoption level unclear. CPR Working Plan (Dec 2025) sequences DPP rollout: Registry Jul 2026, Service Provider rules Q4 2026, CEM first DPP-ready hENs Q4 2027, DWS 2028–2029. STP is NOT in the first DPP cohort. STP DPP timeline likely 2029+.
content.stakeholder_notes: AVCP system: Commission Decision 97/176/EC — System 1 (structural glued products: glulam, LVL, finger-jointed, CLT, load-bearing trusses — certification body required); System 2+ (strength-graded solid timber, wood poles — FPC surveillance by notified body); System 3 (dowel-type fasteners, some connectors — third-party testing). Fire classification: untreated timber typically D-s2,d0; fire-retardant treatment can achieve B-s1,d0. Eurocode 5 (EN 1995) uses charring rate approach for fire design (softwood: 0.65 mm/min one-dimensional). Second-generation EN 1995-1-1:2025 substantially expanded for CLT and LVL — distribution to NSBs by Mar 2026. Environmental & sustainability: EN 16485 is the dedicated EPD PCR for wood/wood-based products (supplements EN 15804+A2). Biogenic carbon accounting under EN 15804+A2 uses the -1/+1 method: carbon uptake credited in modules A1-A3, release assumed at end-of-life in C3/C4 — timber industry argues this undervalues temporary carbon storage during 50-100 year building service life. EUDR (EU Deforestation Regulation) enforcement from Dec 2026 creates parallel supply chain traceability requirements with DPP synergy potential. FSC/PEFC chain-of-custody provides pre-existing digital infrastructure. France RE2020 gives biogenic carbon bonus + 50% timber mandate for public buildings. Nordic countries imposing whole-building carbon limits (Denmark 2023, Sweden/Finland 2025). UK combustibles ban for residential >18m external walls. Germany MBO: timber to 5 storeys, MHolzBauRL updated Sep 2024. ESPR: timber NOT on 2025-2030 priority list.
content.key_risks: (1) Largest standards portfolio: 50 standards (9 hEN + 41 EAD) make coordinated revision and transition planning highly complex. (2) EAD transition scale: 41 EADs expire 9 Jan 2031 — the largest EAD portfolio requiring transition. At 20–30 EADs/year publication rate, this is 1.5–2 years of EOTA capacity for STP alone. (3) Biogenic carbon accounting: EN 15804+A2 -1/+1 method disadvantages long-service-life timber products — policy debate ongoing. (4) M-III (Essential Characteristics) targeted Q1 2026 — status may be stale, verify against Commission/CEN/TC 124 sources. (5) Horizontal dangerous substances SReq (CEN/TC 351) affects timber preservatives, formaldehyde, biocides, VOCs. (6) EUDR enforcement confirmed: 30 Dec 2026 for large/medium operators, 30 Jun 2027 for small/micro. Timber is a regulated commodity. Creates parallel supply chain traceability requirements preceding DPP. No formal DPP-EUDR data linkage mechanism yet. (7) CLT fire safety: susceptible to delamination at elevated temperatures causing cyclical burning. EN 1995-1-2:2025 addresses with CLT-specific charring rules (0.65 mm/min). National regulatory approaches diverge significantly (UK cautious, Austria positive, France mixed). DPP fire performance data may need national context.
content.sources_summary: CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; COM(2025) 772 CPR Working Plan 2026–2029 [S30]; nlfnorm.cz hEN database [S143]; EOTA EAD database [S144]; EOTA transition guidance [S98]. CEI-Bois TIMBIM digital templates (bSDD). Eurocode 5 (EN 1995-1-1:2025). EUDR Regulation (EU) 2023/1115.

standards_summary.source: COM(2025) 772 + CEN/TC 72 + CEN/TC 191 + EOTA PA 10.

content.about: One of the broadest product families, spanning electronic fire detection and alarm systems, fixed sprinkler and gas extinguishing components, smoke and heat control systems, fire dampers, fire hydrants, hose reels, residential sprinklers, and explosion suppression equipment. Four European Assessment Documents cover specialty products. Standardisation is shared across multiple technical committees: CEN/TC 191 (fixed firefighting), CEN/TC 72 (fire detection and alarm), CEN/TC 295 (residential sprinklers), and CEN/TC 305 (explosion suppression), with Euralarm as the key industry body.
content.standards_landscape: 33 harmonised standards cited under old CPR 305/2011 via mandate M/109 (OJ C 092/2018, Implementing Decision (EU) 2019/451). AVCP per Decision 96/577/EC: System 1 for ALL products (most stringent — NB certification required). EN 54 citation backlog: 6+ technically sound standards remain uncited since ~2014 due to James Elliott ruling fallout. Euralarm notes CPR covers "asphalt to electronic systems" under one framework — fire safety needs faster standards update cycles than the current process delivers.
content.standards_development: CEN/TC 72: No active revisions of EN 54 series under new CPR scope. EN 54-1:2021 was a recent editorial update. EN 50130-4 EMC revision ongoing per Euralarm (impacts fire and security industry). CEN/TC 191: WG 5 has NOT begun revising sprinkler hENs — waiting for SReq. CEN/TC 295 (residential sprinklers) and CEN/TC 305 (explosion suppression): No active CPR-relevant projects found.
content.sreq_analysis: Acquis NOT started. M-I targeted Q2 2026, M-III Q1 2027, SReq Q3 2027 per COM(2025) 772 Table 3. No Art. 12 notification found. Under CPR 2024/3110 (DPP applies directly). No standards delivery date set — the breadth of this family (dual TC, ~32 existing hENs) means development will take 3-4 years minimum. EFSN confirms CEN/TC 191/WG 5 has NOT begun revising sprinkler hENs — waiting for the SReq.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2030–2033, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted Q3 2027, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2032–2033. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 33 hENs and 4 EADs range from ~2030 to ~2032 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). The combined range spans from the standards-based assessment’s earlier estimate (~2030–2032) to the policy pipeline’s later horizon (~2032–2033).
content.stakeholder_notes: Multi-directive & environmental: Fire detection products uniquely fall under CPR + EMC (2014/30/EU) + LVD (2014/35/EU) + RoHS (2011/65/EU) + WEEE (2012/19/EU) + Cyber Resilience Act (from Dec 2027). No fire-safety-specific EPD PCR exists — active systems have unique LCA challenges (module B6 operational energy over 15-25 year lifespan, electronic end-of-life). Apollo Fire Detectors targeting EPDs by 2030 using CIBSE TM65 as interim. ESPR: fire safety NOT on priority list but horizontal EEE repairability measures (~2027) could capture electronic detection products. IoT/networked fire systems create product/system boundary challenges: EN 54 certification is per-component, but networked fire alarm systems integrate multiple CPR-covered products with non-CPR software. CRA + CPR + ESPR overlap creates jurisdictional complexity.
content.key_risks: (1) EN 54 citation backlog — 6+ technically sound parts uncited since ~2014 (James Elliott ruling legacy). New CPR Art. 5(8) may resolve but only after full SReq cycle. (2) Multi-regulatory overlap — products fall under CPR + EMC + LVD + RoHS + WEEE + Cyber Resilience Act (from Dec 2027). (3) Acquis not started — M-I targeted Q2 2026 but no evidence of sub-group formation. (4) Breadth of family — 33 hENs across 4 TCs means 3–4 years minimum for standards development. (5) Euralarm estimates 10-year minimum for fire product standard rewriting — the ~2030–2033 DPP range may be optimistic. (6) Cyber Resilience Act (CRA): reporting obligations from Sep 2026, full compliance Dec 2027. Most fire products in self-assessment 'default' category. (8) Zero fire product EPDs — industry must build environmental data infrastructure from scratch for DPP GWP declarations.
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 CPR Working Plan 2026–2029 [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; nlfnorm.cz hEN database [S143]; EOTA EAD database [S144]; Euralarm CPR compliance newsletters (Jan–Feb 2026); EFSN: CEN/TC 191/WG 5 status.

content.about: One of the most fragmented product families, covering internal and external wall cladding, ceiling finishes, and internal partition kits. Products range from fibre-cement sheets, metal cladding, and sandwich panels to suspended ceilings, decorative wallcoverings, natural stone cladding, wood panelling, and high-pressure laminates. Standardisation is spread across seven or more technical committees, primarily CEN/TC 128 (roof and wall cladding), with secondary roles for CEN/TC 277 (suspended ceilings), CEN/TC 241 (gypsum), CEN/TC 99 (wallcoverings), CEN/TC 246 (natural stone), CEN/TC 175 (wood panelling), and CEN/TC 134 (high-pressure laminates).
content.standards_landscape: ~22 hENs cited under CPR 305/2011 via mandate M/121 (OJ C 092/2018, Implementing Decision (EU) 2019/451). AVCP per Commission Decision 98/437/EC (amended by 2001/596/EC): System 1 for reaction-to-fire classes A1–C (NB certification), System 3 for fire classes D–F and fire protection panels, System 4 for other uses (acoustic, thermal, moisture). Significant scope overlap with PF 22 (roof coverings — many standards dual-listed), PF 7 (gypsum — EN 14195/EN 13950), PF 19 (floorings — EN 14411 ceramics), PF 20 (structural metallic — EN 14509 sandwich panels). Note: wallpaper (EN 15102/CEN/TC 99) may be separated into a new family "Decorative paints and wallpapers" under CPR 2024/3110. No EADs in product area 21.
content.sreq_analysis: Not yet adopted. Per COM(2025) 772 Table 3: Milestone I (product scope) targeted Q3 2026, Milestone III (essential characteristics) targeted Q3 2027, standardisation request targeted 2028. No standards delivery or mandatory adoption dates set yet. The future SReq will be issued under CPR 2024/3110.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2032–2033, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted 2028, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2032–2033. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 22 hENs range from ~2032 to ~2033 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). Both scenarios converge on the same timeline.
content.stakeholder_notes: Fire safety & post-Grenfell: Reaction to fire is the dominant performance characteristic — drives AVCP system assignment. New EU facade fire test standard effective Jan 2026 (temperature-based measurement replacing visual assessment, 7 years in development). Post-Grenfell (UK Phase 2 report Sep 2024): UK extending combustible materials ban to buildings >11m (Sep 2025, Euroclass A2 minimum for external facades). 14 EU member states have national fire deviations beyond the Euroclass system. UK Construction Products Reform Green Paper (Feb 2025) proposes a "Construction Library" central repository — proto-DPP concept.

standards_summary.source: COM(2025) 772 + CEN/TC 241 + EOTA PA 7. 14 hENs + 3 EADs.

content.about: Covers all gypsum-based construction products including plasterboards, fibreboards, gypsum blocks, plasters, fibrous casts, composite panels, reprocessed gypsum products, metal framing for drylining systems, and ceiling elements. Two European Assessment Documents address structural/load-bearing plasterboard. A complete drylining or partition system involves boards, framing, fasteners, jointing, and adhesives, each with separate standards. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 241 (Gypsum products).
content.sreq_analysis: Not yet adopted. Per COM(2025) 772 Table 3: Milestone I (product scope) targeted Q3 2026, Milestone III (essential characteristics) targeted Q3 2027, standardisation request targeted 2028. No standards delivery or mandatory adoption dates set yet. The future SReq will be issued under CPR 2024/3110.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2032–2033, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted 2028, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2032–2033. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 14 hENs and 3 EADs range from ~2032 to ~2033 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). Both scenarios converge on the same timeline.
content.stakeholder_notes: AVCP & fire classification: One of the lightest AVCP regimes among all 37 product families. Per Commission Decision 95/467/EC (amended 2002/592/EC): System 3/4 for most products — manufacturer self-declaration. System 1 only for fire-critical products (composite panels, fibrous casts, jointing, metal framing). Gypsum (mineral) classified A1 without testing per Decision 96/603/EC — inherent fire safety guaranteed by material composition (paper liner ≤220 g/m² → A2-s1,d0 per EN 520 Annex B). This is a key competitive advantage: fire performance is a selling point rather than a compliance burden. Circularity & sustainability: Gypsum is 100% recyclable with no property loss, yet only ~13% post-consumer recycled (87% to landfill). Unique challenge: gypsum in mixed landfill produces toxic H₂S gas (UK banned co-disposal 2005). FGD synthetic gypsum declining as Europe decarbonises — urgency to increase C&D recycling. Siniat RECYPLAC: world's first 100% recycled gypsum plasterboard (Jul 2025). EU LIFE GtoG project demonstrated 30% recycled content. Industry: Eurogypsum (EUR 7.7B turnover, 154 quarries, 160 factories). Three European producers — Knauf, Saint-Gobain, Etex — control ~47% of global wallboard capacity. EPDs well-established (IBU Part B PCR for plasterboard). BIM-ready: Knauf Plug-in 2025, Saint-Gobain BIM portal.

standards_summary.source: COM(2025) 772 + CEN/TC 167 + EOTA PA 5 + DIBt hEN list. 6 hENs (EN 1337 parts 3-8) + 8 EADs.

content.about: One of the most compact CPR families, covering structural bearings used in bridges and buildings — including sliding, elastomeric, roller, pot, rocker, spherical PTFE, and guide/restraint bearings — plus pins for structural joints. Six European Assessment Documents address innovative bearing materials, reflecting material innovation outpacing the standard revision cycle. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 167 (Structural bearings). Anti-seismic devices are a separate product family.
content.standards_landscape: 14 standards (6 hEN + 8 EAD). CEN/TC 167 (structural bearings). EN 1337 series covers elastomeric, pot, spherical, roller, and PTFE bearings.
content.standards_development: No active prEN registrations for SBE hENs. EN 1337 series revision will follow SReq adoption. EADs cover niche products: viscous dampers, lock-up devices, seismic isolation bearings.
content.sreq_analysis: Not yet adopted. Per COM(2025) 772 Table 3: Milestone I (product scope) targeted Q4 2026, Milestone III (essential characteristics) targeted Q4 2027, standardisation request targeted 2028. No standards delivery or mandatory adoption dates set yet. The future SReq will be issued under CPR 2024/3110.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2032–2033, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted 2028, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2032–2033. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 6 hENs and 8 EADs range from ~2032 to ~2033 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). Both scenarios converge on the same timeline. Mageba published first verified structural bearings EPD (Dec 2024, RESTON SPHERICAL, HUB-2481): 3.90 kgCO₂e/kg (A1-A3), 33.1% secondary input, 85.7% secondary output. Industry first — sets benchmark for DPP environmental data.
content.stakeholder_notes: Oligopolistic niche — no dedicated European trade association. Key manufacturers: mageba (CH), Maurer (DE), FIP MEC (IT), Freyssinet/VINCI (FR), Trelleborg (SE). DIBt (Germany) is the leading TAB. Market ~USD 180M (Europe). Infrastructure-driven: 200,000+ railway bridges in EU, most >50 years old. Bearings last 30–50 years vs bridge lifespan 75–120 years → replaced 1–3 times per bridge. Demand weighted toward replacement/maintenance. EPD at ground zero: first externally verified EPD published Dec 2024 (mageba RESTON SPHERICAL, EPD Hub). No bearing-specific PCR exists. Global bridge bearings market $3.28B. 10% of EU bridges assessed as deficient. Sensor-integrated structural health monitoring bearings emerging — DPP could carry condition monitoring data alongside product performance.
content.key_risks: (1) PTFE is a PFAS — EU universal PFAS restriction (ECHA proposal) could affect all structural bearings using PTFE sliding surfaces. 7 of 8 EADs cover alternative sliding materials. (2) Niche market with limited EPD infrastructure. (3) Ageing bridge infrastructure (10% EU bridges deficient) creates monitoring urgency.
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; nlfnorm.cz [S143]; EOTA [S144]; mageba RESTON SPHERICAL EPD (HUB-2481, Dec 2024).

content.about: Covers space heating appliances and other heating and cooling appliances, including solid fuel burning residential stoves, roomheaters, cookers, slow heat release appliances, sauna stoves, solid fuel boilers, heat pumps, radiators, convectors, and underfloor heating systems. The scope is broader than the name suggests. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 130 (Space heating appliances).
content.standards_landscape: SHA already has an adopted standardisation request — C(2021) 5359 (adopted 29 Jul 2021), amended Apr 2024 (Art. 12 notification 23 Apr 2024, feedback deadline 9 May 2024). Standards deadlines extended to 31 Dec 2024. Final CEN report due 1 Jun 2025. EN 16510 series is the main standard family. Alignment with Ecodesign Directive 2009/125/EC requirements (thresholds for CO, NOx, OGC, PM emissions; energy efficiency classes). This existing SReq is under old CPR 305/2011 — DPP does not apply to products under these standards (DoP, not DoPC).
content.standards_development: EN 16510 series (9 parts) took effect 9 Nov 2025, replacing 5 older standards (EN 13240, 13229, 12815, 12809, 14785). Manufacturers must re-test entire portfolios. EN 16510-2-7:2025 harmonized via Implementing Decision (EU) 2026/284 (OJ 9 Feb 2026) — coexistence with EN 13240:2001 and EN 14785:2006 ends 9 Feb 2027.
content.sreq_analysis: Table 3 milestones (M-I Q4 2026, M-III Q4 2027, SReq 2028) refer to a future new SReq under the new regulation. Acquis process not yet started. Part also regulated by ESPR — Ecodesign work aligned. DPP will only trigger once new HTS under CPR 2024/3110 replaces old harmonised standards.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2032–2033, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted 2028, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2032–2033. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 10 hENs range from ~2032 to ~2033 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). Both scenarios converge on the same timeline.
content.stakeholder_notes: Pellet stove market: Europe $430M (2025), 58.9% global share, Germany 28.3%. Biomass accounts for ~59% of EU renewable energy consumption — strategic context for SHA. SHA is one of the few families at the direct CPR/ESPR intersection.
content.key_risks: (1) Triple regulation: CPR 2024/3110 + Ecodesign 2015/1185 revision + ESPR 2024/1781 create dual DPP obligation risk. EC identified CPR/Ecodesign discrepancies for solid fuel appliances — resolution pending. (2) CEFACD warns sector crisis: ~11,000 SMEs and 200,000 jobs at risk. No Notified Bodies in Europe notified for EN 16510 conformity assessment. July 2027 Ecodesign compliance date 'completely unrealistic.' European Parliament question E-000487/2025 raised. (3) Zero stove EPDs found under EN 15804 — industry focuses on Ecodesign energy labelling (A++ to G), not lifecycle environmental declarations. DPP GWP data requires entirely new LCA capability.
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 CPR Working Plan 2026–2029 [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; nlfnorm.cz hEN database [S143]. Implementing Decision (EU) 2026/284 (EN 16510-2-7 harmonization). CEFACD position papers. Ecodesign 2015/1185 revision consultation (Dec 2025).
