standards_summary.source: COM(2025) 772 + CEN/TC 128 + CEN/TC 254 + EOTA PA 22.

content.about: One of the richest product families, covering roof coverings (clay tiles, concrete tiles, fibre-cement slates, bitumen sheets, natural slate, metal sheets), roof lights, roof windows, rigid underlays, polycarbonate sheets, sandwich panels, and permanent roof safety anchors. With 32 European Assessment Documents it has the second-highest EAD count across all 37 families, including green roof kits and recycled plastic roofing. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 128 (Roof and wall cladding).
content.standards_landscape: EN 14509 (self-supporting double skin metal faced sandwich panels) is cross-listed in SMP, ROC, and WCF families. Data consistency verified across all three. Implementing Decision 2025/871 (30 Apr 2025) cites EAD 042461-00-1201 (hydrophilic mineral wool for green roofs) and EAD 210195-00-0404 (ultra-thin natural stone veneer sheets) — not yet in ROC standards[]. To be added after scope verification.
content.sreq_analysis: Not yet adopted. Per COM(2025) 772 Table 3: Milestone I (product scope) targeted 2027, Milestone III (essential characteristics) targeted 2027, standardisation request targeted 2028. No standards delivery or mandatory adoption dates set yet. The future SReq will be issued under CPR 2024/3110.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2032–2033, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted 2028, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2032–2033. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 21 hENs and 31 EADs range from ~2032 to ~2033 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). Both scenarios converge on the same timeline.
content.stakeholder_notes: AVCP & fire: Commission Decision 98/436/EC (amended 2001/596/EC) — System 1 for reaction to fire classes A1–C; System 3 for fire classes D–F, durability, water tightness; System 4 for remaining characteristics. Separate Decision 2000/553/EC governs external fire performance of roof coverings (BROOF classification — a characteristic unique to roofing). EN 13501-5 classifies external fire exposure to roofs. Fire is the dominant driver of AVCP complexity: most product standards carry triple systems (1/3/4) depending on the declared fire class. BIPV & green roofs: Building-integrated photovoltaics (BIPV) — solar roof tiles/panels that serve both energy generation and building envelope functions — straddle CPR and ESPR (Ecodesign). No harmonised BIPV standard exists under CPR; CE marking for BIPV as a construction product is not yet possible. Table 3 notes "PV panels regulated by Ecodesign/ESPR, work aligned." Two green roof EADs (220009 and 220078) cover kits with substrate/drainage/vegetation layers — a growing market segment with no hEN route. 32 EADs also include recycled plastic roofing (220010, 220069), sun tunnels (220021), fire-resistant roof windows (220116), and roof windows with integrated balconies (220150). Wienerberger leads EPD readiness (clay roof tiles + Sandtoft In-Roof Solar with BROOF[t4]). BMI Group (largest EU roofing manufacturer) has no EPDs.
content.key_risks: (1) Distant SReq timeline (2028) with acquis not yet started. (2) 31 legacy EADs expire 9 Jan 2031 — only 3 years after planned SReq. No transition planning. (3) EN 14509 (sandwich panels) cross-family consistency risk with SMP and WCF. (4) Fire safety focus (post-Grenfell) — reaction to fire testing dominates AVCP. (5) EPBD mandates solar-ready rooftops: non-residential by Dec 2026, residential by Dec 2029. Massively increases BIPV product demand requiring dual CPR/LVD/EMC compliance. (6) BIPV roof products require dual DPP data (CPR construction + IEC 61215/61730 electrical) — no harmonised approach to combining both in a single passport. (7) EPBD recognises green roofs for first time. EAD 042461 (green roof mineral wool) newly cited. Multi-layer green roof systems create component vs system DPP challenges.
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 CPR Working Plan [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; nlfnorm.cz hEN database [S143]; EOTA EAD database [S144]; Implementing Decision 2025/871.

standards_summary.source: COM(2025) 772 + CEN/TC 226 + CEN/TC 50 + EOTA PA 12.

content.about: Covers safety-critical road infrastructure including vehicle restraint barriers, fixed and variable road signs, traffic signal heads, warning lights, lighting columns, road marking materials, noise barriers, and anti-glare screens. Eleven European Assessment Documents extend the scope into bridge expansion joints and specialty products. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 226 (Road equipment), which also has a newer working group on road infrastructure interactions with autonomous vehicles.
content.standards_landscape: 26 standards (17 hEN + 9 EAD). CEN/TC 226 (road equipment). Mature portfolio with no major revisions expected before SReq.
content.standards_development: CEN/TS 1317-7:2024 (road restraint terminal performance) published — not yet harmonised. No active prEN registrations for CIF hENs under new CPR.
content.sreq_analysis: Not yet adopted. Per COM(2025) 772 Table 3: Milestone I (product scope) targeted 2027, Milestone III (essential characteristics) targeted 2027, standardisation request targeted 2028. No standards delivery or mandatory adoption dates set yet. The future SReq will be issued under CPR 2024/3110.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2032–2033, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted 2028, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2032–2033. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 15 hENs and 11 EADs range from ~2032 to ~2033 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). Both scenarios converge on the same timeline.
content.stakeholder_notes: AVCP & safety: Commission Decision 96/579/EC — System 1 for 80% of standards (12 of 15), reflecting the safety-critical nature of road equipment. Only anti-glare screens and noise barriers carry System 3. No System 4 at all. EN 1317 has 8 parts but only Part 5 is harmonised; Parts 4/7 are under revision (splitting terminal/transition standards), Part 6 is a Technical Report (pedestrian parapets), Part 8 is a Technical Specification (motorcycle restraints). EN 1317-5 demands full-scale vehicle crash tests at speeds up to 110 km/h — one of the most physically demanding test regimes in all of CPR. Milestone 0 already ongoing for a new road restraint assessment method — a unique pre-acquis step where the Commission is developing methodology before formal standardisation begins. Environmental Noise Directive 2002/49/EC creates a parallel demand driver for EN 14388 noise barriers through mandatory noise action plans. Bridge joints & multi-directive: 7 of 11 EADs cover bridge expansion joints (EAD 120011 flexible plug, 120093 asphaltic plug, 120109 nosing, 120110 mat, 120111 cantilever, 120112 supported, 120113 modular) — the family extends well beyond road-surface equipment into bridge infrastructure, overlapping with SBE (structural bearings). Other EADs: microprismatic retroreflective sheeting (120001), wood/metal lighting columns (120003/120017), road marking products (230011). Variable message signs (EN 12966) face multi-directive complexity similar to FFF: EMC Directive 2014/30/EU, Low Voltage Directive 2014/35/EU, and potentially Cyber Resilience Act for connected/IoT signage. The IRMA working group on automated mobility signals future standards for V2I (vehicle-to-infrastructure) road equipment — a growth area for intelligent road infrastructure. EPD adoption early-stage: ArcelorMittal and Unipromet as road barrier EPD pioneers. System 1 certification data infrastructure provides DPP advantage — notified body testing data already digitised. Road safety barriers market ~$1.8B (Europe ~29% global). System 1 compliance cost is significant but provides data infrastructure foundation for DPP.
content.key_risks: (1) EN 12966 (variable message signs): 4-directive compliance burden — CPR + EMC + LVD + Cyber Resilience Act (from Dec 2027). (2) System 1 dominance across most CIF hENs — high conformity assessment cost. (3) Bridge infrastructure overlap with SBE (EADs 120093, 120109, 120112).
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; nlfnorm.cz [S143]; EOTA [S144]; CEN/TC 226 work programme.

standards_summary.source: COM(2025) 772 + CEN/TC 104 + CEN/TC 125 + CEN/TC 303 + CEN/TC 67 + EOTA PA 26.

content.about: Covers concrete admixtures, mineral additions (fly ash, blast furnace slag, silica fume), fibres (steel and polymer), pigments, and the full range of concrete repair products — but notably excludes ready-mixed concrete itself, since the core concrete specification is not harmonised under CPR. Nineteen European Assessment Documents represent the frontier of low-carbon concrete innovation, including steel fibres from end-of-life tyres, FRP reinforcement bars, and calcined clay for low-carbon cements. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 104 (Concrete and related products), with a close relationship to CEN/TC 51 (Cement).
content.standards_landscape: 35 standards (13 hEN + 22 EAD). CEN/TC 104 (concrete), CEN/TC 125 (masonry). Covers concrete admixtures (EN 934 series), repair products (EN 1504 series), grouts (EN 1504-6), screeds, and mortars.
content.standards_development: EN 206-1:2026 ratified (DOR 19 Jan 2026) — potentially transformational if included in CPR SReq scope, covering ready-mixed concrete (~€80B market). Currently non-harmonised. Monitor SReq scope definition.
content.sreq_analysis: SReq not yet started. CMG SReq depends on acquis completion. Cross-family coordination needed with CEM, PCR, and MAS through Concrete Europe (BIBM + CEMBUREAU + EFCA + ERMCO).
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2030–2033, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted Q4 2026 and delivery expected 2029, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2030–2031. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 16 hENs and 19 EADs range from ~2032 to ~2033 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). The combined range spans from the policy pipeline’s earlier estimate (~2030–2031) to the standards-based assessment’s later horizon (~2032–2033).
content.stakeholder_notes: AVCP & complexity: Commission Decision 1999/469/EC — the most AVCP-complex family: EN 1504 repair products carry up to 4 different systems (1, 2+, 3, 4) depending on the characteristic. System 1+ for mineral additions (fly ash, slag, silica fume — safety-critical as they modify concrete strength). System 2+ for admixtures (EN 934 series) and pigments. System 1/3 for fibres (structural vs non-structural use). System 4 only for non-structural surface protection. The acquis work (Milestone I: ongoing, Milestone III: Q1 2026) could bring EN 206 into CPR scope for the first time — potentially transformational for the ~EUR 80B European ready-mixed concrete market. Circularity & innovation: 19 EADs show strong circular economy innovation: EAD 260010 (steel fibres recovered from end-of-life tyres), EAD 260009 (MSWI bottom ash as concrete addition), EAD 260023 (carbon/glass/basalt/aramid FRP reinforcement bars — alternative to steel), EAD 260014 (calcined clay as type II addition — key for LC3 low-carbon cements), EAD 260020 (catalytic calcium carbonate), EAD 260006 (polymeric addition), EAD 260035 (natural calcined pozzolana). Also includes FRP structural sections (260001), inorganic fibre grid reinforcement (260057), and zinc anode cathodic protection systems (260100). Many of these EADs represent the frontier of low-carbon concrete technology. Concrete Europe (BIBM + CEMBUREAU + EFCA + ERMCO) coordinates DPP data model consistency across the concrete value chain: CEM, CMG, PCR, MAS families.
content.key_risks: (1) EN 206 scope expansion risk: if included in SReq, standards development doubles in complexity. (2) Split EPD readiness: EFCA admixtures excellent (EPDs since 2005), EN 1504 repair products lacking. (3) 22 legacy EADs expire 9 Jan 2031.
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; nlfnorm.cz [S143]; EOTA [S144]; EFCA admixture EPD programme.

content.sreq_analysis: Not yet adopted. Per COM(2025) 772 Table 3: Milestone I (product scope) finished, Milestone III (essential characteristics) targeted Q2 2026, standardisation request targeted Q4 2026, standards delivery targeted 2029. EN 13830 is expected to be revised under the future SReq. Curtain walling accessories are also referenced in the DWS draft SReq (Jan 2026). The future SReq will be issued under CPR 2024/3110.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2030–2032, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted Q4 2026 and delivery expected 2029, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2030–2031. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 1 hEN and 25 EADs range from ~2030 to ~2032 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). The combined range spans from the standards-based assessment’s earlier estimate (~2030–2032) to the policy pipeline’s later horizon (~2030–2031).
content.stakeholder_notes: Schüco, Reynaers, ALUMIL, WICONA all publish EN 15804+A2 EPDs — among strongest EPD coverage in CPR. Aluminium curtain walls benefit from 95% energy saving in recycling. Global facades market $298B (2025), curtain walls 45% of European facade value.

Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 2 hENs range from ~2030 to ~2031 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). Both scenarios converge on the same timeline.
content.stakeholder_notes: FEICA Model EPDs (since 2016, updated 2022/2024 with IVK, Deutsche Bauchemie, EFCC): formulation-based, EN 15804+A2 verified, covering ~800 companies (90% SME). Sika has product-specific EPDs since 2012. ADH among the most DPP-ready families via collective FEICA approach. Deutsche Bauchemie: 140 members, €4.6B sales, active on CPR policy. content.about references EN 12860 (gypsum adhesives), structural bonding, and floor covering adhesives — none currently tracked in standards[]. Review scope alignment when M-I (2027) defines product scope. Global tile adhesive market $3.6B, flooring adhesive $5.6B. SReq scope expansion could significantly increase ADH's regulatory footprint.
content.key_risks: (1) EN 12004-1 AVCP mismatch: ADH lists System 3, CMG lists System 3/4 — 6th cross-family AVCP discrepancy identified. Verify against Commission Decision for PA 25/26. (2) REACH diisocyanate restriction (Aug 2023): PU adhesives with >0.1% require mandatory user training. DPP must declare diisocyanate content and training requirements. Market shifting to non-PU alternatives. (3) CEN/TC 351 dangerous substances horizontal SReq affects adhesive formulations (VOCs, formaldehyde, isocyanates).

standards_summary.source: COM(2025) 772 + EOTA PA 35. 0 hENs (product standards — CEN/TC 127 develops test methods only). 6 EADs covering fire stopping, sealing, protective coatings, and retardant products.

content.about: Covers fire stopping and sealing products such as penetration seals and joint seals, reactive coatings, fire protective boards and mats, and fire retardant treatments. Key standard series include EN 13381 (fire protection to structural elements), EN 1366 (fire resistance of service installations), and EN 1634 (fire resistance of door/shutter assemblies), all under AVCP System 1. Standardisation is led by CEN/TC 127 (Fire safety in buildings).
content.standards_landscape: FPP is an EAD-only family: no hENs. 6 EADs cover fire stopping (penetration seals, linear joint/gap seals), fire sealing (intumescent products), fire protective products (reactive coatings for structural steel), and fire retardant treatments. All based on former ETAGs 018 and 026. Over 800 ETAs have been issued. AVCP System 1 for all products. CEN/TC 127 develops fire test methods (EN 13381, EN 1366, EN 1634 series) but not product standards under CPR.
content.standards_development: No harmonised product standards under development. CEN/TC 127 continues fire safety test method work (EN 13501 series). Product standardisation for fire stopping/sealing would be new territory — currently all market access is via EAD/ETA route.
content.sreq_analysis: Not yet adopted. No acquis sub-group formally established. Per COM(2025) 772 Table 3: Milestone I (product scope) targeted 2028, Milestone III (essential characteristics) targeted 2028, standardisation request targeted 2029. No standards delivery or mandatory adoption dates set yet. The future SReq will be issued under CPR 2024/3110.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2030–2032, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted 2029, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2030–2031. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 6 EADs converge on ~2032. The combined range spans from the policy pipeline’s earlier estimate (~2030–2031) to the standards-based assessment’s later horizon (~2032).
content.stakeholder_notes: Fire stopping market $1.7B (2024), CAGR 8.9%, Europe >30%. 800+ active ETAs confirmed by EOTA. FPP is the bellwether for EAD-only family transition under CPR 2024/3110 — lessons will inform FIX, KAS, and other EAD-heavy families. Split EPD readiness: intumescent coatings have EPDs (Nullifire SC802–804, Sherwin-Williams Firetex M71V2, Rudolf Hensel 410KS–421KS). Penetration seals and linear gap seals likely lack EPDs. Hilti BIM/digital infrastructure (Button for Firestop, Documentation Manager, NBS Source CFS-CT, CAD/BIM Typicals) demonstrates fire stopping products can be structured digitally — DPP data architecture groundwork from digital-ready manufacturers.
content.key_risks: (1) Timeline gap: EADs expire 9 Jan 2031 but SReq not planned until 2029. Only ~2 years for the full SReq→development→citation cycle — typically 3–4 years. ETA backstop to 2036 mitigates but new ETA issuance stops in 2031. (2) EAD-only dependency: FPP has no hEN fallback. If hEN route doesn't materialise in time, products face a regulatory gap. (3) PFAS exposure: EU PFAS restriction may affect EAD 350865 (fire retardants). ECHA SEAC consultation spring 2026, final opinions end 2026. (4) Dual chemical reformulation pressure: PFAS + ECHA brominated flame retardant restriction (pending). Reformulated products may require new ETAs. (5) CEN/TC 127 develops test methods, not product standards — capacity for product standardisation under new CPR is untested.
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 CPR Working Plan 2026–2029 [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; EOTA EAD database [S144]. ETAG 018/026: former guidelines used as EADs. Regulation (EU) 2025/1988 (PFAS).

content.sreq_analysis: Not yet adopted. Acquis sub-group established. Per COM(2025) 772 Table 3: Milestone I (product scope) ongoing, Milestone III (essential characteristics) targeted Q2 2026, standardisation request targeted Q1 2027. No standards delivery or mandatory adoption dates set yet. The EN 771 series and EN 998 series are expected to be revised under the future SReq. The future SReq will be issued under CPR 2024/3110.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2031–2032, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: With standardisation request targeted Q1 2027, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2031–2032. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 11 hENs and 10 EADs range from ~2031 to ~2032 (all hENs currently cited under CPR 305/2011, requiring revision under CPR 2024/3110 before DPP applies). Both scenarios converge on the same timeline.
content.stakeholder_notes: Wienerberger leads EPD readiness (clay bricks). TBE (European brick body) requires cradle-to-grave EPDs. Concrete blocks, AAC, and calcium silicate lag. Wienerberger electric kiln at Uttendorf achieves 90% CO₂ reduction.
content.key_risks: (1) Material diversity: 6 masonry material types with different EPD readiness. (2) EAD 170005 (recycled clay), EAD 170051 (compressed earth), EAD 170012 (cellular glass core) represent circular economy innovation but EADs expire 2031. (3) AAC market growing (CAGR 5.5%) but EPD infrastructure lags.
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; nlfnorm.cz [S143]; EOTA [S144]; TBE masonry position; Wienerberger sustainability reports.

standards_summary.source: COM(2025) 772 + EOTA PA 33. 0 hENs + 34 EADs for post-installed fasteners.

content.about: Covers mechanical fasteners such as anchors, bolts, and screws, as well as chemical anchors, post-installed rebar connections, and channel bolt systems for fixing to concrete, masonry, and other substrates. This family is primarily covered through European Technical Assessments (ETAs) issued by EOTA.
content.standards_landscape: EAD-only family: 0 hENs, 34 EADs for post-installed fasteners. 5,500+ ETAs issued — the largest ETA market segment. All under EOTA working area, primarily based on former ETAG 001. EAD 330031 (plastic anchors for ETICS) connects to KAS family — cross-family tracking needed for ETICS kit/fastener coordination.
content.standards_development: No harmonised product standards under development. Product assessment is entirely via EAD/ETA route. EN 1992-4:2018 (Eurocode 2 Part 4) provides design rules that depend on ETA performance data. New CPR requires machine-readable EAD format for BIM/DPP integration.
content.sreq_analysis: According to the Commission Working Plan (Table 3), Milestone I (product scope) and Milestone III (essential characteristics) are both planned for 2028. No standardisation request date has been set yet. Since fixings rely primarily on the ETA route (EADs rather than hENs), the transition depends on how EOTA adapts its assessment documents to the new CPR framework.
content.dpp_outlook: The estimated DPP range is ~2032–2033, informed by two scenarios. Policy pipeline: Based on the Commission Working Plan, the regulatory pathway points to DPP by ~2032–2033. Standards-based: Per-standard DPP estimates across 34 EADs converge on ~2032. The combined range spans from the standards-based assessment’s earlier estimate (~2032) to the policy pipeline’s later horizon (~2032–2033).
content.stakeholder_notes: EPD readiness highly concentrated: Hilti has ~300 product declarations including EPDs. Fischer and Würth have no visible EPD programmes. c-PCRs (complementary Product Category Rules) for fixings LCA under development. CBAM applies to imported steel fasteners from Jan 2026. New CPR requires machine-readable EAD format — enabling BIM and DPP data integration for the 5,500+ active ETAs.
content.key_risks: (1) EAD transition bottleneck: ~400 EADs across all families vs ~25/yr EOTA publishing rate = 16 years needed vs 3-year window. Industry warns of 'innovation freeze' and return to national MS assessments. Systemic risk for all EAD-heavy families. (2) Seismic performance categories C1/C2 (ETAG 001 Annex E) are safety-critical DPP data fields — using C1 where C2 required = structural failure risk in earthquake zones.
content.sources_summary: COM(2025) 772 CPR Working Plan 2026–2029 [S30]; CPR 2024/3110 [S1]; EOTA EAD database [S144]; EOTA transition guidance [S98]. CFE trade press (EAD bottleneck analysis). EN 1992-4 (Eurocode 2 Part 4).
